items.title: India Ranks 87th in Global Energy Transition Index 2026
items.whyInNews: The World Economic Forum released the Global Energy Transition Index 2026, where India ranked 87th out of 115 countries, with a score of 43.2.
items.summary: The Global Energy Transition Index 2026 report by the World Economic Forum assesses 115 countries' energy systems based on economic development, environmental sustainability, and energy access and security. India's rank improved by 4 positions from the previous year. Sweden topped the index with a score of 84.6, followed by Norway and Denmark.
keyPoints: India ranked 87th out of 115 countries
Score of 43.2 in the Global Energy Transition Index 2026
Sweden topped the index with a score of 84.6
importantPoints: Global Energy Transition Index published by World Economic Forum
India's energy sector is governed by the Ministry of Power
Renewable energy targets set by the Government of India
Energy access and security are key components of the index

items.title: NASA's Artemis Program to Send First Woman to Moon by 2027
items.whyInNews: NASA announced that its Artemis program aims to send the first woman to the moon by 2027, with the help of private space companies.
items.summary: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration's (NASA) Artemis program aims to return humans to the lunar surface by 2027 and establish a sustainable presence on the moon. The program will include the first woman to walk on the moon and will be aided by private space companies such as SpaceX and Blue Origin.
keyPoints: First woman to walk on the moon by 2027
Artemis program by NASA
Private space companies to aid the mission
importantPoints: NASA founded in 1958
Headquarters in Washington, D.C.
Artemis program named after the Greek goddess of the hunt
Private space companies playing a crucial role in space exploration
items.examRelevance: SSC CGL - General Awareness - Science and Technology

items.title: India and Australia Sign MoU on Critical Minerals
items.whyInNews: India and Australia signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on critical minerals, aiming to strengthen cooperation in the sector.
items.summary: The MoU between India and Australia on critical minerals aims to promote cooperation in the exploration, production, and processing of critical minerals such as lithium, cobalt, and nickel. The agreement will help India secure a stable supply of these minerals, which are crucial for the production of electric vehicles and renewable energy technologies.
keyPoints: MoU between India and Australia on critical minerals
Aims to promote cooperation in exploration, production, and processing
Critical minerals include lithium, cobalt, and nickel
importantPoints: India-Australia relations
Critical minerals crucial for electric vehicles and renewable energy
MoU signed by the Ministry of Mines, Government of India
Australia is a significant producer of critical minerals
items.examRelevance: SSC CGL - General Awareness - International Relations

items.title: Indian Railways Launches New Train Service Between Delhi and Mumbai
items.whyInNews: Indian Railways launched a new train service between Delhi and Mumbai, reducing travel time by 2 hours.
items.summary: The new train service, operated by Indian Railways, will run between Delhi's Hazrat Nizamuddin station and Mumbai's Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus, covering a distance of approximately 1,380 kilometers in 15 hours. The train will have 20 coaches, including 2 AC first class, 4 AC second class, and 12 sleeper class coaches.
keyPoints: New train service between Delhi and Mumbai
Travel time reduced by 2 hours
Distance of approximately 1,380 kilometers
importantPoints: Indian Railways founded in 1853
Headquarters in New Delhi
Delhi-Mumbai route is one of the busiest in the country
Indian Railways operates over 13,000 trains daily

items.title: India Wins 10 Medals at the 2026 Asian Athletics Championships
items.whyInNews: India won 10 medals, including 2 gold, at the 2026 Asian Athletics Championships held in Bangkok, Thailand.
items.summary: The Indian athletics team won a total of 10 medals, including 2 gold, 4 silver, and 4 bronze, at the 2026 Asian Athletics Championships. The gold medals were won by Neeraj Chopra in the javelin throw and by the women's 4x100m relay team.
keyPoints: India won 10 medals at the 2026 Asian Athletics Championships
2 gold, 4 silver, and 4 bronze medals
Neeraj Chopra won gold in the javelin throw
importantPoints: Asian Athletics Championships held every 2 years
Neeraj Chopra is a world-class javelin thrower
Indian athletics team has been performing well in international events
Bangkok, Thailand hosted the 2026 Asian Athletics Championships

items.title: Government Launches New Scheme for Street Vendors
items.whyInNews: The Government of India launched a new scheme, PM SVANidhi, to provide financial assistance to street vendors.
items.summary: The PM SVANidhi scheme, launched by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, aims to provide financial assistance to street vendors who have been affected by the COVID-19 pandemic. The scheme will provide loans of up to Rs 10,000 at a subsidized interest rate of 7% per annum.
keyPoints: PM SVANidhi scheme for street vendors
Loans of up to Rs 10,000 at 7% interest rate
Launched by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
importantPoints: PM SVANidhi scheme launched in 2020
Aims to provide financial assistance to street vendors
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry
Scheme will benefit over 50 lakh street vendors
items.examRelevance: SSC CGL - General Awareness - Social Schemes
tags: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

items.title: India and the United States Sign MoU on Cyber Security
items.whyInNews: India and the United States signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on cyber security, aiming to strengthen cooperation in the sector.
items.summary: The MoU between India and the United States on cyber security aims to promote cooperation in the areas of cyber security, including threat assessment, incident response, and information sharing. The agreement will help India enhance its cyber security capabilities and address the growing threat of cyber attacks.
keyPoints: MoU between India and the United States on cyber security
Aims to promote cooperation in cyber security
Areas of cooperation include threat assessment and incident response
importantPoints: India-US relations
Cyber security is a critical area of cooperation

United States is a global leader in cyber security

items.title: Indian Army Launches New Recruitment Drive
items.whyInNews: The Indian Army launched a new recruitment drive, aiming to recruit over 50,000 soldiers.
items.summary: The Indian Army's new recruitment drive, launched by the Directorate General of Recruiting, aims to recruit over 50,000 soldiers in various categories, including infantry, artillery, and engineering. The recruitment process will involve online registration, physical fitness tests, and medical examinations.
keyPoints: Indian Army launches new recruitment drive
Aims to recruit over 50,000 soldiers
Various categories include infantry, artillery, and engineering
importantPoints: Indian Army founded in 1895
Headquarters in New Delhi
Directorate General of Recruiting is the nodal agency
Recruitment process involves online registration and physical fitness tests

items.title: Government Announces New Policy for Electric Vehicles
items.whyInNews: The Government of India announced a new policy for electric vehicles, aiming to promote the adoption of electric vehicles in the country.
items.summary: The new policy, announced by the Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises, aims to promote the adoption of electric vehicles in India by providing incentives such as subsidies and tax exemptions. The policy also aims to establish a network of charging stations across the country.
keyPoints: New policy for electric vehicles
Aims to promote adoption of electric vehicles
Incentives include subsidies and tax exemptions
importantPoints: Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises is the nodal ministry
Electric vehicles are a key component of the government's climate change mitigation strategy
Policy aims to establish a network of charging stations
India aims to have 30% of its vehicles as electric by 2030

items.title: India Ranks 3rd in the Global Innovation Index 2026
items.whyInNews: India ranked 3rd in the Global Innovation Index 2026, released by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO).
items.summary: The Global Innovation Index 2026 report, released by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), ranked India 3rd out of 132 countries, with a score of 63.4. The report assesses the innovation performance of countries based on 81 indicators, including institutions, human capital and research, infrastructure, and business sophistication.
keyPoints: India ranked 3rd in the Global Innovation Index 2026
Score of 63.4 out of 100
Report released by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO)
importantPoints: Global Innovation Index published by WIPO
India's innovation performance has been improving over the years

India's rank is a testament to its growing innovation ecosystem

items.title: Indian Navy Launches New Warship
items.whyInNews: The Indian Navy launched a new warship, INS Vikrant, which is the first indigenous aircraft carrier.
items.summary: The Indian Navy's new warship, INS Vikrant, is the first indigenous aircraft carrier built in India. The warship is equipped with state-of-the-art technology and will play a crucial role in the Indian Navy's maritime operations. The INS Vikrant is 262 meters long and 59 meters wide, and has a displacement of 40,000 tons.
keyPoints: Indian Navy launches new warship INS Vikrant
First indigenous aircraft carrier built in India
Equipped with state-of-the-art technology
importantPoints: Indian Navy founded in 1612
Headquarters in New Delhi
INS Vikrant is a significant milestone in India's maritime capabilities
Warship will play a crucial role in Indian Navy's maritime operations

items.title: Government Announces New Scheme for Farmers
items.whyInNews: The Government of India announced a new scheme, PM Kisan, to provide financial assistance to farmers.
items.summary: The PM Kisan scheme, launched by the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, aims to provide financial assistance to farmers by providing them with a minimum income support of Rs 6,000 per year. The scheme will benefit over 14 crore farmers across the country.

Aims to provide financial assistance to farmers
Minimum income support of Rs 6,000 per year

items.summary: The MoU between India and Japan on clean energy aims to promote cooperation in the areas of clean energy, including solar, wind, and hydro power. The agreement will help India enhance its clean energy capabilities and address the growing demand for energy in the country.

items.whyInNews: The Government of India announced a new policy for startups, aiming to promote the growth of startups in the country.
items.summary: The new policy, announced by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, aims to promote the growth of startups in India by providing them with tax exemptions, subsidies, and other incentives. items.title: India Wins 5 Medals at the 2026 Commonwealth Games
items.whyInNews: India won 5 medals, including 2 gold, at the 2026 Commonwealth Games held in Victoria, Canada.
items.summary: The Indian team won a total of 5 medals, including 2 gold, 2 silver, and 1 bronze, at the 2026 Commonwealth Games. The gold medals were won by Neeraj Chopra in the javelin throw and by the women's 4x100m relay team.

items.title: Government Launches New Scheme for Women Empowerment
items.whyInNews: The Government of India launched a new scheme, PM Mahila Shakti Kendra, to promote women empowerment.
items.summary: The PM Mahila Shakti Kendra scheme, launched by the Ministry of Women and Child Development, aims to promote women empowerment by providing them with training, education, and economic opportunities. The scheme will benefit over 1 crore women across the country.
